title: Understanding the Problem
text: The biker is launched at an angle of $$60$$ degrees and lands $$45.0$$ $$m$$ away, $$20$$ $$m$$ below the launch point. We need to find the initial speed.

title: Breaking Down the Motion
text: The motion can be broken into horizontal and vertical components. Use trigonometry to find these components.

title: Horizontal Motion
text: The horizontal distance covered is $$45.0$$ $$m$$. Use the formula: $$horizontal_{distance}$$ $$=$$ $$initial_{horizontal,velocity}$$ * time.

title: Vertical Motion
text: The vertical motion is affected by gravity. Use the formula: $$vertical_{displacement}$$ $$=$$ $$initial_{vertical,velocity}$$ * time - $$0.5$$ * g * $${time}^2$$.

title: Using Trigonometry
text: The initial horizontal velocity is $$initial_{speed}$$ * cos(60) and the initial vertical velocity is $$initial_{speed}$$ * sin(60).

title: Finding Time of Flight
text: Use the vertical motion equation to solve for time: $$20$$ $$=$$ $$initial_{speed}$$ * sin(60) * time - $$0.5$$ * $$9.8$$ * $${time}^2$$.

title: Solving for Initial Speed
text: Use the horizontal motion equation: $$45$$ $$=$$ $$initial_{speed}$$ * cos(60) * time. Substitute the time found in the previous step.
